payload.reason: Low design ambiguity: all 4 design forks (judge-criteria=max-finding-severity, per-role blocking_severity default major, tier-wiring deferred, pass-with-open needs no new events) resolved in brainstorm and user-approved; change is additive single-field governance + one reject predicate, no gate/hot-path change. Dogfooding risk-matched intensity per Step-2; code review runs governance-required 2 sources.
automated review round frozen for caller-owned execution

checklist.note: The pass-with-open-finding guarantee is false for automated plan review, and the prepared governance payload omits the new threshold.
All changed files are within the declared implementation-plan scope.
The frozen event path is coherent, but the draft packet exposes an incomplete governance representation.
Automated plan-reviewer rounds can approve minor findings without retaining them as open findings.
The public governance example and configuration description do not document blocking_severity.

findings.summary: The new severity predicate applies to plan-reviewer rounds too, so an automated plan verdict with a failed checklist and only minor findings is approved. However, derive_open_findings and the report intentionally ingest only code-reviewer results. The plan finding therefore does not remain open or surface in the report, contradicting the feature's honesty guarantee and reviewer prompt. Restrict pass-with-open-finding to code review or add durable plan-finding tracking and tests.

_governance_payload omits role.blocking_severity. Prepared packets therefore present an incomplete review_governance snapshot even though the threshold affects their prompt and contract, contrary to Task 2's instruction to mirror the field in the sibling governance payload.

The declared scope omits the public governance documentation. docs/getting-started.md still presents the canonical review-governance YAML without blocking_severity, and docs/concepts.md lists the governed role properties without the new threshold, leaving operators without documentation for the configurable behavior.

payload.reason: Expand scope to document blocking_severity for adopters (init template + getting-started), addressing the code review's doc-impact concern

Unchanged rationale from first pass: low-ambiguity design fully resolved in brainstorm; the scope expansion only adds documentation of blocking_severity (init template comment + getting-started example), no design change. Dogfooding risk-matched intensity; code review runs governance-required 2 sources on the final head.
automated review round frozen for caller-owned execution
Complete adopter docs per code-review CODX-003: also document blocking_severity in docs/concepts.md governed-role property list (getting-started + init template already done)
Doc-only scope completion (concepts.md governed-role property list); no design change; disclosed skip consistent with prior passes
automated review round frozen for caller-owned execution

checklist.note: Reject predicate keys on max finding severity vs frozen per-role blocking_severity; scope_sufficient hard-rejects; plan review unchanged; frozen-governance mirrors min_independent (absent->minor).
Edits confined to the 13 declared files; no new event types / derive_open_findings / merge-gate changes.
CODX-001 fix gates severity predicate to reviewer=='code-reviewer'; else keeps checklist-fail reject for plan-reviewer. aggregate['findings'] always a list; prompt gated via pass_with_open.

scope_sufficient=False rejects in both branches; absent severity->minor; honesty law verified (approved minor still in derive_open_findings); plan minor correctly rejects via real prepare->begin->import path.
init template comment + getting-started example/prose + concepts.md property list document blocking_severity, default, pass-with-open, and plan-review exemption.

prior_findings.note: _close_round_if_terminal gates the severity predicate to code-reviewer; plan-reviewer keeps checklist-fail reject; prompt sentence gated; regression tests exercise the real plan path.
_governance_payload now includes blocking_severity; test_review_prepare asserts it.
init template + getting-started + concepts.md all document blocking_severity and its semantics.

checklist.note: Failed-source retries do not honor the new pass-with-open severity policy when deciding which successful peer results to retain.
The plan's declared scope still omits four files changed by this target.
The new severity parsing can raise an uncaught TypeError for malformed non-scalar values.
Partial producer failure and malformed severity inputs are not handled consistently.
Public governance documentation now describes blocking_severity and the code-review-only behavior.

findings.summary: retained_sources still treats every failing checklist as unsuccessful. If one source reports only minor findings under the default major threshold and another source crashes, the otherwise non-blocking result is discarded and the retry reruns both sources. This defeats the new pass-with-open policy, existing peer-receipt retention, and the feature's review-cost goal.

The declared scope still omits changed files docs/concepts.md, docs/getting-started.md, src/super_harness/cli/init.py, and tests/unit/cli/test_review_prepare.py, so the implementation exceeds its plan artifact's stated file scope.

A non-scalar YAML value such as blocking_severity: [major] is tested directly for set membership and raises TypeError instead of ReviewGovernanceError. The same pattern in review_runs.py can crash state derivation for a malformed event payload; both paths should require a string before membership testing.

prior_findings.note: Pass-with-open is now restricted to code-reviewer rounds, with a regression test confirming minor plan findings still reject.
_governance_payload now includes role.blocking_severity and the prepared-packet test asserts it.
Public documentation was updated, but the plan scope omission remains; that remaining issue is re-reported as CODX-002 in this run.

print(state.retained_sources)
PY

failed_checks.command: PATH="$(pwd)/.venv/bin:$PATH" ruff check src tests scripts

payload.suggested_fix: Re-run the failed checks locally, fix the underlying issues, then re-run `super-harness verify`.

payload.reason: automated review round frozen for caller-owned execution

checklist.note: verdict_blocks matches design contract; shared by close and succeeded so they never diverge.
All edits in scope; plan doc scope list refreshed (CODX-002).

checklist.note: Human-confirmed code reviews do not honor the severity threshold.
The plan's final scope includes every file in the frozen target.
The human path duplicates and contradicts the shared verdict_blocks predicate.
Human-only and mixed-source governance remain incorrect.
Documentation describes a general code-review policy that the human workflow does not implement.

findings.summary: Human-confirmed code reviews still reject on any checklist failure via failing_items instead of applying the role's blocking_severity. Since init defaults to a human-only reviewer, a minor-only human finding still forces rejection. The duplicated _run_approved predicate at line 2477 also excludes otherwise non-blocking automated receipts from mixed-source quorum. The advertised pass-with-open policy therefore does not work for the default human workflow.

prior_findings.note: retained_sources now uses the shared verdict_blocks predicate with each round's frozen threshold, retaining minor-only code-review results across peer execution failures.
The plan's final declared scope now lists docs/concepts.md, docs/getting-started.md, src/super_harness/cli/init.py, tests/unit/cli/test_review_prepare.py, and all other target files.
Governance parsing now requires blocking_severity to be a string before membership testing, and review-run derivation safely defaults malformed non-scalar payload values to minor.

checklist.note: verdict_blocks is the single reject predicate at all four deciders.
Changes within declared files.
Severity rank order; scope handled inside verdict_blocks; aggregate collects all sources' findings.
Frozen reader defaults minor; governance default major; human path uses live role for a fresh round.
init/getting-started/concepts + reviewer prompt document the policy; plan-review-unchanged noted.

prior_findings.note: Both human-path deciders call verdict_blocks with role.blocking_severity; minor->approve / major->reject tests pass; failing_items removed with no remaining references.

checklist.note: Mixed-source quorum does not preserve the prior round's frozen severity policy.
A governance threshold change between automated review and human confirmation can reverse whether an existing receipt counts.

findings.summary: Mixed human quorum re-grades every prior automated receipt using the current role.blocking_severity instead of the threshold frozen on prior_round. If governance is relaxed after an automated round rejected, that rejecting receipt can become “approved” and satisfy quorum without a new automated review; tightening policy can conversely discard a receipt that passed its frozen round. Pass prior_round.blocking_severity into the predicate, and freeze the threshold on human review_round_started events as well so all receipts retain their opening policy.

prior_findings.note: Human outcome calculation and ordinary mixed-source quorum now use verdict_blocks, so minor-only human and automated findings follow blocking_severity. The remaining frozen-policy issue is recorded separately.
